description_long: This object is a vintage corded telephone with a robust, rectangular black base and a cream-colored handset resting on top. The base features a prominent rotary dial with circular finger holes and a transparent center, typical of mid-20th-century telephones. The handset is thick and ergonomically curved, designed to fit comfortably against the ear and mouth. The cradle on the base securely holds the handset in place when not in use, and the rotary dial is slightly angled for ease of access. The materials appear to be primarily hard plastic with some internal metal components for structural support and electrical function. The overall design is utilitarian yet classic, evoking a sense of nostalgia and reliability.
description: A classic black and cream rotary dial corded telephone with a sturdy base and curved handset, featuring a prominent rotary dial and cradle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, cradle, and the full width of the handset.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the handset and the angled rotary dial.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the handset and the solid, sloped base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handset profile and the rotary dial's side.
